dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 5992 IH: Stuck On Hold Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Calvert (for himself, Mr. Van Orden, Mr. Barrett, Mr. Ciscomani, Mr. Webster of Florida, and Ms. King-Hinds) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs

form.official-title: To direct the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to implement automated systems with callback functionality for each customer service telephone line of the Department of Veterans Affairs, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Stuck On Hold Act.

section.header: Improvements regarding wait times for callers to certain service telephone lines of the Department of Veterans Affairs

subsection.text: Not later than one year after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall implement, for each covered line, an automated system that—

paragraph.text: informs any caller to a covered line about the anticipated wait time, if any; and
automatically offers a callback to any such caller with an anticipated wait time of more than 10 minutes.

subsection.text: The Secretary shall issue such guidance the Secretary determines necessary to reduce the average wait time of a caller to a covered line to not more than 10 minutes.
In this section, the term covered line means a customer service telephone line of the Department of Veterans Affairs. Such term does not include—

paragraph.text: the toll-free hotline for veterans provided by the Secretary under section 1720F(h) of title 38, United States Code; or
a phone line for the emergency department of a health care facility of the Department.
